codeReview.summary: Implementation is functionally correct but has a critical commit reference mismatch. The anthropic.rs file (lines 1-214) implements chat(), tool support, error handling, and health_check() correctly. All 3 anthropic tests pass and clippy shows no warnings. However, the handoff references commit f6ed1d2 which is actually the MCP client implementation in swell-tools, not the anthropic backend.

issues.description: Handoff references wrong commit. commitId 'f6ed1d2' is 'feat(swell-tools): implement MCP client for external tool servers', not the anthropic backend. The anthropic backend was actually implemented in commit '3a8c017 feat: complete Rust backend skeleton'.
Feature description mentions 'streaming' but implementation uses standard request/response without streaming. The expectedBehavior does not explicitly require streaming, so this may be acceptable.

No test coverage for: (1) API error handling paths (non-2xx responses), (2) JSON parse failure handling, (3) actual API communication (only mock-style unit tests exist). The existing tests only verify model name and role conversion.
health_check() uses HEAD request to API URL. This is a simple implementation that may not catch all connection issues (e.g., invalid API key).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature specifies skillName 'rust-worker' but this skill doesn't exist at .factory/skills/rust-worker/SKILL.md. The actual skill is at .factory/skills/worker/SKILL.md. Worker encountered error 'Skill "rust-worker" not found' and transcript shows worker was confused by this.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json has skillName: 'rust-worker' but only .factory/skills/worker/SKILL.md exists. Transcript shows: 'Error: Skill "rust-worker" not found'

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker appears to have successfully completed the feature despite the skill mismatch. The implementation follows correct Rust conventions (async_trait, tracing, thiserror imports available in swell-core). However, the AGENTS.md doesn't specify where to find skill definitions or what to do when a skill is missing.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Implementation in anthropic.rs follows proper patterns. Transcript shows worker worked around skill mismatch.

summary: Implementation is functionally correct with all tests passing. Blocking issue: handoff references wrong commit (f6ed1d2 instead of 3a8c017). Non-blocking: streaming mentioned in description but not implemented (though not in expectedBehavior), and limited test coverage for error paths. Skill name mismatch caused confusion but worker completed work successfully.
